The **NRL Under-20s** (known commercially as the **Holden Cup** due to sponsorship from [Holden](/wiki/Holden "Holden")) was the top league of professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") for players aged 20 years or younger in [Australasia](/wiki/Australasia "Australasia"). Contested by sixteen teams, the Under-20s competition commenced in 2008 and was originally known as the **Toyota Cup**.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The competition runs parallel to Australasia's professional competition, the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"), with NYC matches played immediately prior to the NRL games.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Similar to the NRL, the NYC enforces a [salary cap](/wiki/Salary_cap "Salary cap") and puts a heavy focus on life outside football for the players.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The [New Zealand Warriors](/wiki/New_Zealand_Warriors "New Zealand Warriors") were the most successful club in the competition's short history, with three premierships from four Grand Final appearances; in 2010, 2011 and 2014. In 2018, the NRL Under-20s was replaced by state-based under-20s competitions in [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") and [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NRL_Under-20s&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The NRL Under-20s succeeded the [Jersey Flegg Cup](/wiki/Jersey_Flegg_Cup "Jersey Flegg Cup") in 2008, which existed from 1961 to 2007. The competition was administered by the [New South Wales Rugby League](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Rugby_League "New South Wales Rugby League") as an under-19s competition, until it was changed to an under-20s competition in 1998.
On October 28, 2016 it was announced that the 2017 season will be the last for the NRL Under-20s. It was to be replaced by stronger State-based competitions in NSW and QLD, these being the reformed [Jersey Flegg Cup](/wiki/Jersey_Flegg_Cup "Jersey Flegg Cup") in NSW and the new [Hastings Deering Colts](/wiki/Hastings_Deering_Colts "Hastings Deering Colts") in Queensland.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NRL_Under-20s&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Awards")]

### Player of the Year

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NRL_Under-20s&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Player of the Year")]

The National Youth Competition Player of the Year award is the premier individual award in the National Youth Competition. The voting for the award is similar to the [Dally M Medal](/wiki/Dally_M_Medal "Dally M Medal") voting, where after each National Youth Competition game 3 points are awarded to the best player on ground, 2 points to the second and 1 point to the third. As of 2017, every winner of the award has gone on to play first grade in the [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"). The inaugural winner was [Ben Hunt](/wiki/Ben_Hunt_(rugby_league) "Ben Hunt (rugby league)") from the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") in 2008. Hunt is also the youngest player to win the award, at age 18 years, 5 months and 13 days.

### Jack Gibson Medal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NRL_Under-20s&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Jack Gibson Medal")]

The Jack Gibson Medal is awarded to the man of the match of the Toyota Cup grand final. The award is named after legendary [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") coach, [Jack Gibson](/wiki/Jack_Gibson_(rugby_league) "Jack Gibson (rugby league)"). Gibson, who guided [Eastern Suburbs](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") to premierships in 1974 and 1975, the [Parramatta Eels](/wiki/Parramatta_Eels "Parramatta Eels") to three successive premierships from 1981 to 1983 and was named coach of the Team of the Century, died in 2008.

## Television coverage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NRL_Under-20s&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Television coverage")]

### Australia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NRL_Under-20s&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Australia")]

* Free to air: [Channel 9](/wiki/Nine_Network "Nine Network") showed the Grand Final as part of the Grand Final Coverage.[[13]](#cite_note-2007_Big_League_Annual_page_11-13)
* Subscription television: FOX Sports show 2 games live every weekend, live coverage of the Toyota Cup precede Fox Sports' [Super Saturday](/wiki/Super_Saturday "Super Saturday") and Sunday live NRL coverage.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

### New Zealand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NRL_Under-20s&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: New Zealand")]

* All New Zealand Warriors home games in the U20's competition are shown live by [Sky NZ](/wiki/SKY_Network_Television "SKY Network Television").[[13]](#cite_note-2007_Big_League_Annual_page_11-13) [Māori Television](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_Television "Māori Television") also broadcasts *Ngāti NRL*, a series that focuses on young Māori and Pacific Islanders who travel to Australia and play in the Toyota Cup.[[15]](#cite_note-15)
